raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"A review of this section centrally includes assessing whether the thesis, gap, and contribution claims are framed appropriately.","R1.2":"The reviewer is expected to check whether the argument from method to benchmark results to clinical utility is internally coherent.","R1.3":"The section makes quantitative performance and calibration claims, so reviewing whether the stated evidence supports those claims is central.","R1.4":"The artifact contains explicit claims about prior approaches and comparable calibration, making novelty assessment a central review responsibility.","R1.5":"Because the request targets a whole section, section-level narrative order and progression are part of the expected review.","R1.6":"A section review normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, register, and concision.","RX.1":"The projection should preserve uncertainty about what can be inferred from the section alone.","RX.2":"The review responsibility includes identifying and avoiding unsupported claims of clinical usefulness, novelty, or superiority.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within reviewing the section rather than executing edits or broader research tasks.","RX.4":"Because the artifact concerns clinical-genetics triage, review responsibilities include avoiding downstream harm from overstated or unsafe claims.","RX.5":"The review should attend to traceability from claims to reported benchmarks, figures, and cited prior work."}}

rationale.R1.1: A review of this section centrally includes assessing whether the thesis, gap, and contribution claims are framed appropriately.
rationale.R1.2: The reviewer is expected to check whether the argument from method to benchmark results to clinical utility is internally coherent.
rationale.R1.3: The section makes quantitative performance and calibration claims, so reviewing whether the stated evidence supports those claims is central.
rationale.R1.4: The artifact contains explicit claims about prior approaches and comparable calibration, making novelty assessment a central review responsibility.
rationale.R1.5: Because the request targets a whole section, section-level narrative order and progression are part of the expected review.
rationale.R1.6: A section review normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, register, and concision.
rationale.RX.1: The projection should preserve uncertainty about what can be inferred from the section alone.
rationale.RX.2: The review responsibility includes identifying and avoiding unsupported claims of clinical usefulness, novelty, or superiority.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within reviewing the section rather than executing edits or broader research tasks.
rationale.RX.4: Because the artifact concerns clinical-genetics triage, review responsibilities include avoiding downstream harm from overstated or unsafe claims.
rationale.RX.5: The review should attend to traceability from claims to reported benchmarks, figures, and cited prior work.
